channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 3 large bin bags cluttering the pavement at the top of the street, 1st July
item.description:
Three large bin bags full of what appears to be garden waste cluttering the pavement. It’s really difficult for parents with pushchairs to get past.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gunton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous fridge freezer left on pavement in front of number 18, 1st July
item.description:
Large fridge freezer with a door that keeps opening and looks like it could topple over. It’s a danger to small children on bikes and scooters and needs removing immediately.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gunton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant drug taking, 1st July
item.description:
Bike stores and area around garden constantly used for drug dealers and drug taking by a group 5/6 strong. Multiple reports to police. Southwark must liase now. Two bike stores and bike racks in a small area create a private space for exactly this. Review area layout and create more open spaces for bike storage that incorporates the large open pedestrian area. Request visible police and security presence.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): King's Grove, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip of masonry and a microwave, 1st July
item.description:
Flytip of the above on Hazelbourne Road near the junction with cavendish Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hazelbourne Road, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The trees are sprouting new growth from the bottom, 1st July
item.description:
The trees all along hurst Road , between the roundabout and Holy Trinity were “pruned “ earlier this year. There is a mass of growth from the bottom of the trees and up the trunks. The upper parts are not a major problem but the low growth spreads into the pavements and severely blocks the view of residents turning out into the road and of the pavement when turning in from the road. The tree near Chis and Sid school is particularly bad covering about a metre of pavement and on a bend is dangerously for the children crossing the road. Please could you rectify this asap and ask the contracted tree surgeons to have a more efficient management plan. The trees look as if they have been badly hacked and without having to maintain upper tree growth and hydration are sprouting growth all over.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 171, Hurst Road, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree is falling down, 1st July
item.description:
Risk of damage to people and properties

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Park Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping all over, 1st July
item.description:
Disgusting behaviour but also total lack on street cleaning. Rubbish on the tree has been there for weeks of not months

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Park Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Too near to my front gate, 1st July
item.description:
The is park to near to my front gate that I'm scared that I will scratched the car and please maybe put a raid marking clearer cause I think there is marking but it's fadedaleady.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Smedley Street, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Beoken concret post, 1st July
item.description:
Lorry's to big for road destroying pavements across the road and on the side with the damage post

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wolsley Close, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Alongside corner house, 1st July
item.description:
Open box broken door since at least the weekend

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fordwych Road, Kilburn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Highway obstruction, 1st July
item.description:
Lodos supermarket keeps storing bottles and watermelons on the pavement obstructing the way

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hackney Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Combermere rd covered in litter, 1st July
item.description:
Daily littering by delivery drivers and taxi drivers who use Combermere rd to rest. Also affected by pupils and parents who drop litter on the road when dropping children off at Lansdowne school and Stockwell primary school. Patrons from fast food premises on Stockwell rd also drop litter on Combermere rd. Requires more frequent litter picking. Litter pickers not collecting all rubbish when they do attend. Street requires more frequent litter collection and better oversight of the limited litter picking that does happen. Issue escalated to Lambeth multiple times.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Stockwell Green, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping corner of stillness x Crofton Park road, 1st July
item.description:
Various items dumped on corner including sofas, bins and mattresses

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stillness Road, Honor Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lethal shrubs over hanging foot path, 1st July
item.description:
Reported this last week nothing had been cut back. You can now clearly see the lethal thorns in this shrub. That flew into my granddaughters face today . This is disgraceful and dangerous. That area of colyers lane is an accident waiting to happen including the low branches. Over the foot path from 42. And the dangerous low lying branches in the school field .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Colyers Lane, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More flyposting - Marxism, 1st July
item.description:
More flyposting - Marxism

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 35, St George's Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing recycling bins for 1 week, 1st July
item.description:
More again today - Recycling bin area littered with garbage bags and filth again. It’s smelly, filthy attracting vermin and flies, flying into residential properties and causing noise pollution during the night

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Haberdasher Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 1st July
item.description:
Household items and sofas dumped as well as rubbish bags

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Majendie Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two vans not moved for months, 1st July
item.description:
These two vans appear abandoned as they have not moves for over 2 months, the larger J20 MF5 has straps on its soft size, when vehicles go by they clatter and bang loudly

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hichisson Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog poo, 1st July
item.description:
Dog poo by rubbish bag next to overgrown tree growth at base. All of these are issues to be fixed.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Culford Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Roadway little, 1st July
item.description:
Thank you for cleaning up Cradley rd but you appear to have missed Beaverbank rd as previously mentioned with cradley rd. please can you address this issue with Beaverbank.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cradley Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet
